name: Chef Kimos' Rack of Lamb
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F to 375°F.
2. Place slivers of garlic along the bones and base of the rack.
3. Season all over with salt and pepper.
4. Heat a small amount of oil in a skillet until very hot.
5. Quickly brown the meaty top side of the rack only; do not allow it to cook or burn.
6. Transfer the rack to a baking dish.
7. While the oven is preheating, prepare the sauce by heating it just below boiling point while stirring constantly. Use it to baste the rack and as a serving sauce.
8. Place the baking dish in the center of the preheated oven.
9. Pour half of the sauce over the rack of lamb.
10. Bake for about 20-25 minutes per pound, depending on thickness.
11. Serve with the remaining sauce and enjoy the praise from your family and friends.
Note: This sauce can also be used as a barbecue sauce.
